Laburnum nigricans is a woody shrub that grows up to 10 meters in height and has compound leaves with 5-7 leaflets. The plant produces clusters of yellow flowers in late spring, which are highly fragrant and attractive to bees and other pollinators. Laburnum nigricans is also known for its toxicity, as all parts of the plant contain toxic alkaloids.
